## Working Directory

Work only in `D:/CLAUDE/Project/unified-crm-finance`. Do not `cd` elsewhere. Relevant context is preloaded; start without re-reading it.

## Executor Context Constraints

The agent that executes each task has a **200K token** context window.
- Recommended task count for this slice: **2–6 tasks**
- Each task gets ~320K chars of inline context (plans, code, decisions)
- Keep individual tasks completable within a single context window — if a task needs more context than fits, split it

## UNIT: Plan Slice S02 ("Project API + Repository") — Milestone M004

## Context Mode

Lane: **planning lane**.
Use `gsd_resume` for planning continuity, `gsd_exec` for noisy checks, and `gsd_exec_search` before rerunning diagnostics.

## Context Snapshot

# GSD context snapshot (2026-06-21T23:19:33.948Z)

## Top project memories
- [MEM019] (gotcha) Prisma 7.x breaks Prisma 6.x schema format: datasource `url` property moved to prisma.config.ts. Task specified Prisma 6.x, so locked apps/web to Prisma 6.6.0 to avoid breaking changes.
- [MEM069] (gotcha) Project.id requires manual UUID generation via randomUUID() - no @default in schema. Project.updatedAt must be manually set on update - no @updatedAt attribute. Same pattern as Deal, Contact, and other existing models.
- [MEM071] (pattern) Next.js API routes use App Router: route.ts files export async GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE functions. Collection endpoints at /api/resource/, single resource at /api/resource/[id]/route.ts. Returns NextResponse.json({ data: result }) for success, { error, message } for errors.
- [MEM009] (architecture) Prisma schema.prisma is single source of truth; SQLAlchemy uses automap_base() reflection, not declarative models. Python worker re-reflects schema on restart after Prisma migrations.
- [MEM014] (gotcha) S01 summary describes files (apps/web, docker-compose.yml, package.json) that don't actually exist in the repo. Current state has only apps/worker with FastAPI. Plan S06 CI/CD for actual code, not S01 summary claims. Web app CI/CD jobs will be added when apps/web is created later.
- [MEM018] (architecture) Using SQLite for development instead of PostgreSQL due to Docker Desktop unavailable. Schema datasource is sqlite (not postgresql). This is a temporary dev-only setup; production will use PostgreSQL.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

## Handoff from research-slice

**Intent:** Completed research-slice for M004/S02

### Milestone Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M004/M004-ROADMAP.md`

# M004: Проекты

**Vision:** Модуль управления проектами — параллельная lifetime со сделкой система для tracking исполнения контрактов. Проект создаётся на этапе "Подписан контракт" в сделке и существует независимо от коммерческого pipeline сделки.

## Success Criteria

- Пользователь может создать проект из сделки (на этапе контракта) и увидеть его в списке проектов
- Gantt timeline показывает этапы проекта с корректными датами и цветовой кодировкой по статусу
- Пользователь может создать Production сущность, добавить этапы производства, и tracking процесс до поставки на объект
- File upload работает: drag & drop, preview, удаление. Чертежи прикрепляются к сделке, спецификации к проекту
- Cascade close: акт закрывает проект → сделка закрывается автоматически (или наоборот)

## Slices

- [x] **S01: S01** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: ProductionRepository passes all unit tests (CRUD + softDelete), Prisma migration applies successfully

- [ ] **S02: Project API + Repository** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: ProjectRepository passes unit tests (CRUD, softDelete, count), ProjectApiClient can create/read/update/delete via curl

- [ ] **S03: Project List + Create UI** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: User can browse /dashboard/projects, filter by status/manager, create project via modal, see project cards in table

- [ ] **S04: Project Detail Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02,S03]`
  > After this: User can view /dashboard/projects/[id], see project info, stages list, team members with roles, related deal/contract

- [ ] **S05: Gantt Timeline** `risk:high` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: User can see Gantt chart on project detail, drag dates to update, color coding by status, day-level zoom

- [ ] **S06: Production Management** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01,S04]`
  > After this: User can create Production records (PLATE/COUNTERTOP), manage production stages, see status in project detail

- [ ] **S07: File Upload + Cascade Close** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04,S06]`
  > After this: User can drag-drop files to attach (drawings to deals, specs to projects), preview works, delete works; closing project with act cascades to deal

## Boundary Map

## Scope Boundaries\n\n### In Scope\n- Project CRUD (создание из сделки + отдельно)\n- ProjectStage pipeline (8 стандартных этапов)\n- ProjectMember management (множественные роли)\n- Gantt timeline (vis-timeline, drag-drop, цвет по статусу)\n- Production entity + ProductionStage (PLATE, COUNTERTOP)\n- File upload UI (drag-drop, preview, delete)\n- File attachments: чертежи к сделкам, акты к сделкам, спецификации к проектам\n- Cascade close: акт ↔ проект ↔ сделка\n\n### Out of Scope\n- Task Manager (организационные задачи) — отдельная сущность\n- Gantt advanced features (critical path, resource leveling, export PDF) — M007\n- Production planning (BOM optimization) — M005\n- Финансовая отчетность по проектам — M006\n\n### Integration Points\n- Deal: создание проекта на этапе контракта, cascade close\n- Contract: привязка проекта к контракту\n- Contact: клиент проекта\n- FileEntity: storage для чертежей, актов, спецификаций\n- User/Role: команда проекта, RBAC\n- BOM/BOMItem: спецификации проекта (уже существуют)

---

### Slice Research
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M004/slices/S02/S02-RESEARCH.md`

# Research: Slice S02 - Project API + Repository

## Summary

Slice S02 requires implementing the Project CRUD layer: ProjectRepository in the backend, ProjectApiClient in the frontend, and Next.js API routes. The Prisma schema for Project, ProjectStage, and ProjectMember already exists. ProductionRepository was completed in S01 and serves as a reference. The implementation should follow the established DealRepository pattern for consistency.

## Recommendation

Create ProjectRepository (`src/lib/db/projects.ts`) mirroring DealRepository structure, with methods for CRUD, softDelete, and stage management. Create ProjectApiClient (`src/lib/api/projects.ts`) following DealApiClient pattern with fetch wrapper and typed methods. Add API routes at `src/app/api/projects/` and `src/app/api/projects/[id]/`. Add ProjectData, ProjectStageData, ProjectMemberData types to `src/lib/api/types.ts`. Write unit tests for repository and API client.

## Implementation Landscape

### Key Files to Create

| File | Purpose | Pattern to Reuse |
|------|---------|------------------|
| `apps/web/src/lib/db/projects.ts` | ProjectRepository class | DealRepository structure |
| `apps/web/src/lib/db/projects.test.ts` | Repository unit tests | DealRepository tests, ProductionRepository tests |
| `apps/web/src/lib/api/projects.ts` | ProjectApiClient class | DealApiClient structure |
| `apps/web/src/lib/api/projects.test.ts` | API client tests | DealApiClient tests |
| `apps/web/src/app/api/projects/route.ts` | GET/POST /api/projects | Deal collection route |
| `apps/web/src/app/api/projects/[id]/route.ts` | GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/projects/[id] | Deal single resource route |
| `apps/web/src/lib/api/types.ts` | Add Project* types | ContactData, DealData pattern |

### Existing Schema (Prisma)

```prisma
// Project model (lines 686-728)
model Project {
  id              String            @id
  externalNumber  String            @unique
  name            String
  description     String?
  dealId          String?           @unique
  contractId      String?           @unique
  contactId       String?
  status          String            @default("lead")
  contractAmount  Float             @default(0)
  currency        String            @default("RUB")
  startDate       DateTime?
  endDate         DateTime?
  completedAt     DateTime?
  marginTarget    Float             @default(0.25)
  qualityRating   String?
  deadlineStatus  String            @default("on_track")
  managerId       String?
  attributes      Json?
  createdAt       DateTime          @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime
  deletedAt       DateTime?
  // Relations: User, Contact, ProjectMember[], ProjectStage[], Production?, etc.
}

// ProjectStage model (lines 744-760)
model ProjectStage {
  id          String    @id
  projectId   String
  code        String
  name        String
  order       Int
  status      String    @default("pending")
  startDate   DateTime?
  endDate     DateTime?
  completedAt DateTime?
  assigneeId  String?
  notes       String?
  // @@unique([projectId, code])
}

// ProjectMember model (lines 730-742)
model ProjectMember {
  id        String    @id
  projectId String
  userId    String
  role      String
  joinedAt  DateTime  @default(now())
  leftAt    DateTime?
  // @@unique([projectId, userId])
}
```

### Build Order

1. **Repository**: Create `ProjectRepository` in `src/lib/db/projects.ts`
   - CRUD methods: findMany, findUnique, create, update, softDelete, count
   - Query methods: findByStatus, findByManager, findByContact, findByDeal
   - Stage methods: createStage, updateStage, moveStage (optional for S02)
   - Member methods: addMember, removeMember, findMembers (optional for S02)

2. **Repository Tests**: Create `projects.test.ts` alongside repository
   - CRUD tests with test fixtures
   - Soft-delete behavior tests
   - Count tests

3. **API Types**: Add to `src/lib/api/types.ts`
   - ProjectData (with relations)
   - ProjectStageData
   - ProjectMemberData
   - ProjectFilters, ProjectListParams
   - ProjectCreateInput, ProjectUpdateInput

4. **API Routes**: Create `src/app/api/projects/`
   - `route.ts`: GET (list with filters), POST (create)
   - `[id]/route.ts`: GET (single), PATCH (update), DELETE (soft delete)

5. **API Client**: Create `ProjectApiClient` in `src/lib/api/projects.ts`
   - getProjects, getProject, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject
   - Fetch wrapper with parseApiError, parseJson

6. **API Client Tests**: Create `projects.test.ts` alongside API client
   - Mock fetch tests for each method
   - Error handling tests

### Verification Approach

1. **Repository Tests**: Run `npx tsx --test src/lib/db/projects.test.ts`
   - Verify CRUD operations work
   - Verify soft-delete excludes from queries
   - Verify count works correctly

2. **API Tests**: Run `npx tsx --test src/lib/api/projects.test.ts`
   - Verify fetch serialization
   - Verify error handling

3. **Integration Test** (manual via curl):
   ```bash
   # Create project
   curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/projects \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"name":"Test Project","externalNumber":"TEST-001"}'
   
   # List projects
   curl http://localhost:3000/api/projects
   
   # Get single project
   curl http://localhost:3000/api/projects/[id]
   
   # Update project
   curl -X PATCH http://localhost:3000/api/projects/[id] \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"status":"active"}'
   
   # Delete project
   curl -X DELETE http://localhost:3000/api/projects/[id]
   ```

## Don't Hand-Roll

| Feature | Use Existing |
|---------|--------------|
| Repository pattern | DealRepository (`src/lib/db/deals.ts`) |
| API client pattern | DealApiClient (`src/lib/api/deals.ts`) |
| API route structure | Deal routes (`src/app/api/deals/`) |
| Fetch utilities | ApiClientError, parseApiError, parseJson (`src/lib/api/shared.ts`) |
| Test patterns | DealRepository tests, DealApiClient tests |
| Type definitions | DealData, DealCreateInput pattern in `types.ts` |

## Constraints

- Prisma 6.6.0 (locked in package.json)
- SQLite for development (no PostgreSQL)
- Next.js 16, React 19
- TypeScript strict mode
- Soft-delete pattern (deletedAt field)
- Russian language UI (labels in Russian)

## Common Pitfalls

1. **Project number generation**: Project uses `externalNumber` (unique string), not auto-generated like Deal's `number`. Need to decide if API should auto-generate or require from client.

2. **Member unique constraint**: ProjectMember has `@@unique([projectId, userId])`. Adding duplicate member for same user+project should error gracefully.

3. **Stage code uniqueness**: ProjectStage has `@@unique([projectId, code])`. Creating duplicate stage codes for same project should error.

4. **Cascade deletes**: Project deletion cascades to ProjectStage, ProjectMember (onDelete: Cascade). Verify this works correctly in tests.

5. **include patterns**: When fetching Project with relations, use Prisma include syntax matching DealRepository patterns (include: { ProjectStage: true, ProjectMember: true, etc.}).

6. **updatedAt not auto-managed**: Project model has `updatedAt DateTime` without `@updatedAt`. Must manually set on update (like DealRepository does).

7. **id requires manual generation**: Project.id is `@id` with no @default, must use `randomUUID()` on create.

## Open Risks

- **externalNumber format**: Spec unclear on format. Use string from client for now, can add auto-generation later.
- **Stage workflow**: 8 standard stages defined in milestone but not seeded in DB. API should allow dynamic stage creation.
- **Member multi-role**: One user can have multiple roles via multiple ProjectMember records (no unique constraint on projectId+userId+role). UI needs to handle this.
- **Cascade close**: Aкт → project/deal cascade logic is S07 scope. S02 only needs basic CRUD.

## Sources

- Schema: `apps/web/prisma/schema.prisma` (Project lines 686-728, ProjectStage 744-760, ProjectMember 730-742)
- Reference repository: `apps/web/src/lib/db/deals.ts` (DealRepository pattern)
- Reference API: `apps/web/src/lib/api/deals.ts` (DealApiClient pattern)
- Reference routes: `apps/web/src/app/api/deals/route.ts`, `apps/web/src/app/api/deals/[id]/route.ts`
- Reference types: `apps/web/src/lib/api/types.ts` (DealData, DealCreateInput patterns)
- Production reference: `apps/web/src/lib/db/production.ts` (completed in S01)
- Package constraints: `apps/web/package.json` (Prisma 6.6.0, Next.js 16)
- Memory: MEM022 (Project UUID, updatedAt manual), MEM063 (multi-role members)

---

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions (compact)
Fields: id | when | scope | decision | choice | rationale | revisable | made_by

D021 | M004 planning | architecture | Production как отдельная сущность | Production — это отдельная model в Prisma схеме (1:N к Project) | Производство имеет собственный pipeline (этапы до поставки на объект), может быть несколько на один проект, и отличается от основных этапов проекта. Типы: PLATE (плитные материалы), COUNTERTOP (столешницы из кварца/акрила). | Yes | agent
D022 | M004 planning | architecture | ProjectMember с мульти-ролями | Один пользователь может иметь несколько ролей в одном проекте через множественные ProjectMember записи | В одной команде пользователь может быть одновременно и менеджером, и технологом, и исполнителем. Это business requirement, а не ошибка данных. | Yes | agent
D023 | M004 planning | library | Gantt реализация с vis-timeline | Использовать библиотеку vis-timeline для MVP Gantt | vis-timeline проверена, работает без тяжелых зависимостей, покрывает MVP requirement. Critical path, resource leveling, export PDF — отложены на M007. | Yes | agent
D024 | M004 planning | integration | File attachments через FileEntity | Использовать существующую FileEntity model с MinIO storage | FileEntity уже существует в схеме. Не нужно новой инфраструктуры. Просто нужна UI интеграция. | Yes | agent
D025 | M004 planning | architecture | Cascade close логика | Акт выполненных работ может закрыть либо проект, либо сделку. Другая сущность закрывается автоматически (cascade). | Гибкость для разных business процессов. Иногда акт = завершение проекта (сделка follows), иногда акт = завершение сделки (проект follows). | Yes | agent

---

### Dependency Slice Summaries

Use Forward Intelligence from dependencies when present.

- (no dependencies)

## Mission

Plan this slice against real code and persist it through the DB-backed tool.

Use `subagent` only under `planning-dispatch` for isolated planning reconnaissance: broad subsystem scouting, unclear decomposition, or current external facts. For external research, dispatch the **scout** agent. Do not dispatch implementation agents.

Before planning, validate roadmap assumptions against code and dependency summaries. If concrete evidence shows downstream slices are wrong, call `gsd_reassess_roadmap` with `sliceChanges.modified`, `sliceChanges.added`, or `sliceChanges.removed`; otherwise keep the roadmap unchanged. Bias toward "roadmap is fine." Completed slices are immutable.

### Source Files

- **Project**: `.gsd/PROJECT.md`
- **Requirements**: `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md`
- **Milestone Context**: `.gsd/milestones/M004/M004-CONTEXT.md`
- **Roadmap**: `.gsd/milestones/M004/M004-ROADMAP.md`
- **Slice Research**: `.gsd/milestones/M004/slices/S02/S02-RESEARCH.md`

If slice research is inlined, trust its architectural findings, but verify every concrete file path you place in task `inputs` or `expectedOutput` against the current tree or prior/same-task outputs. Explore enough code to confirm paths, boundaries, and verification. Executors later get only task plans, slice excerpt, and prior summaries, so put required paths, steps, inputs, and outputs in task plans.

## Planning Rules

1. If requirements are preloaded, identify owned and supporting Active requirements.
2. Call `memory_query` with keywords from the slice title and source files.
3. Read `C:\Users\Yura\.gsd\agent\extensions\gsd\templates\plan.md` and `C:\Users\Yura\.gsd\agent\extensions\gsd\templates\task-plan.md`.
4. <skill_recommendations unit="plan-slice">For this unit type, also consider invoking: api-design, decompose-into-slices, design-an-interface, grill-me, tdd, verify-before-complete, write-docs. Use Skill({ skill: 'name' }) when relevant — these are recommendations, not requirements.</skill_recommendations> Record expected executor skills in each task plan's `skills_used` frontmatter.
5. Define slice verification before tasks. Non-trivial slices need real tests or executable assertions; boundary contracts need contract-exercising checks. Tests must not read .gitignore/gitignored paths such as `.gsd/`, `.planning/`, or `.audits/`.
6. Include Threat Surface (Q3), Requirement Impact (Q4), proof level, observability, integration closure, Failure Modes (Q5), Load Profile (Q6), and Negative Tests (Q7) only where applicable.
7. Right-size tasks. Simple slices can be one task; split only when context, ownership, or verification boundaries justify it.
8. Each task needs the exact `gsd_plan_slice.tasks[]` shape: `taskId`, `title`, `description`, `estimate`, `files`, `verify`, `inputs`, `expectedOutput`, and optional `observabilityImpact`. `description` should contain the Why / Do / Done-when narrative. `files`, `inputs`, and `expectedOutput` must be JSON arrays of strings, even when there is only one path (for example, `"inputs": ["src/index.ts"]`, never `"inputs": "src/index.ts"`). Use paths relative to `D:/CLAUDE/Project/unified-crm-finance`; do not put absolute paths to the original checkout or any directory outside `D:/CLAUDE/Project/unified-crm-finance` in `files`, `inputs`, `expectedOutput`, or verification commands. **`expectedOutput` must only list files the task actually creates or overwrites on disk.** Do NOT include files the task merely reads, verifies, or tests — those belong only in `inputs`. If a task is a pure verification or test task that produces no new files, `expectedOutput` may be `[]` or limited to test-result artifacts (e.g. a log or assertion output). A file that does not yet exist on disk and is needed as an `input` must be produced by an earlier task's `expectedOutput` — if no prior task creates it, add a task before this one that does.
9. Persist with `gsd_plan_slice` using `milestoneId`, `sliceId`, `goal`, optional `successCriteria`/`proofLevel`/`integrationClosure`/`observabilityImpact`, and `tasks`. `gsd_plan_slice` handles task persistence transactionally and renders `D:\CLAUDE\Project\unified-crm-finance\.gsd\milestones\M004\slices\S02\S02-PLAN.md` plus task plans; do not call `gsd_plan_task`. The DB-backed tool is the canonical write path. Do **not** rely on direct `PLAN.md` writes as the source of truth.
10. Self-audit before finishing: goal/demo closure, requirement coverage, locked decisions, concrete paths, dependency order, wiring, scope size, proof truthfulness, feature completeness, and quality gates. Quality gates: non-trivial slices/tasks include specific Q3-Q7 coverage where applicable.
11. If planning creates structural decisions, call `gsd_decision_save` for each; the tool persists the decision and regenerates `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`.
12. Do not commit — .gsd/ planning docs are managed externally and not tracked in git.

The slice directory already exists. Do not mkdir.

**Autonomous execution:** no human is available. Do not call `ask_user_questions` or `secure_env_collect`; make reasonable assumptions and document them.

**You MUST call `gsd_plan_slice` to persist planning state before finishing.**

When done, say: "Slice S02 planned."

    11→## Threat Surface

    15→- **Abuse**: {{abuseScenarios — parameter tampering, replay, privilege escalation, or N/A}}
    16→- **Data exposure**: {{sensitiveDataAccessible — PII, tokens, secrets, or none}}

    26→
    27→## Proof Level

    34→
    35→## Verification
    36→
    37→<!-- Define what "done" looks like BEFORE detailing tasks.
    38→     This section is the slice's objective stopping condition — execution isn't done
    39→     until everything here passes.
    40→
    41→     For non-trivial projects:
    42→     - Write actual test files into the codebase during the first task
    43→     - Tests should assert on the slice's demo outcome and boundary contracts
    44→     - Name the test files here so execution has an unambiguous target
    45→
    46→     For simple projects or scripts:
    47→     - Executable verification commands (bash assertions, curl checks, etc.) are sufficient
    48→
    49→     If the project has no test framework and the work is non-trivial,
    50→     the first task should set one up. A test runner costs 2 minutes
    51→     and pays for itself immediately.
    52→
    53→     For non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI work:
    54→     - Include at least one verification check for an observability or failure-path signal
    55→     - Verify not just that the feature works, but that a future agent can inspect its state when it fails -->
    56→
    57→- {{testFileOrCommand — e.g. `npm test -- --grep "auth flow"` or `bash scripts/verify-s01.sh`}}
    58→- {{testFileOrCommand}}
    59→
    60→## Observability / Diagnostics
    61→
    62→<!-- Include this section for non-trivial backend, integration, async, stateful, or UI slices.
    63→     OMIT ENTIRELY for simple slices where all fields would be "none".
    64→
    65→     When included, describe how a future agent will inspect current state, detect failure,
    66→     and localize the problem with minimal ambiguity. Keep it concise and high-signal. -->

    69→- Inspection surfaces: {{status endpoint, CLI command, script, UI state, DB table, or none}}
    70→- Failure visibility: {{last error, retry count, phase, timestamp, correlation id, or none}}
    71→- Redaction constraints: {{secret/PII boundary or none}}
    72→
    73→## Integration Closure
    74→
    75→<!-- Omit this section entirely for simple slices with no meaningful integration concerns. -->
    76→
    77→- Upstream surfaces consumed: {{specific files / modules / contracts}}
    78→- New wiring introduced in this slice: {{entrypoint / composition / runtime hookup, or none}}
    79→- What remains before the milestone is truly usable end-to-end: {{list or "nothing"}}
    80→
    81→## Tasks
    82→
    83→<!--
    84→  If every task below is completed exactly as written, the Goal and Demo above
    85→  should be true at the stated proof level. Tasks should close the loop on the
    86→  slice, not merely prepare for later work unless the Demo truthfully says the
    87→  slice only proves fixture/contract-level behavior.
    88→
    89→  Write each task as an executable increment, not a vague intention.
    90→
    91→  Prefer action-oriented titles:
    92→  - "Wire real auth middleware into dashboard routes"
    93→  - "Persist job status and expose failure diagnostics"
    94→  - "Add browser test covering empty-state recovery"
    95→
    96→  Avoid vague titles:
    97→  - "Set up auth"
    98→  - "Handle errors"
    99→  - "Improve UI"
   100→
   101→  Each task should usually include:
   102→  - description: why this task exists, concrete steps, and done-when acceptance
   103→  - files: JSON array of likely touched paths
   104→  - verify: the command, test, or runtime check that proves it worked
   105→  - inputs: JSON array of existing paths or prior task outputs this task consumes
   106→  - expectedOutput: JSON array of paths this task creates or overwrites
   107→
   108→  Keep the checkbox line format exactly:
   109→  - [ ] **T01: Title** `est:30m`
   110→-->
   111→
   112→- [ ] **T01: {{taskTitle}}** `est:{{estimate}}`
   113→  - Why: {{whyThisTaskExists}}
   114→  - Files: `{{filePath}}`, `{{filePath}}`
   115→  - Do: {{specificImplementationStepsAndConstraints}}
   116→  - Verify: {{testCommandOrRuntimeCheck}}
   117→  - Done when: {{measurableAcceptanceCondition}}
   118→- [ ] **T02: {{taskTitle}}** `est:{{estimate}}`
   119→  - Why: {{whyThisTaskExists}}
   120→  - Files: `{{filePath}}`, `{{filePath}}`
   121→  - Do: {{specificImplementationStepsAndConstraints}}
   122→  - Verify: {{testCommandOrRuntimeCheck}}
   123→  - Done when: {{measurableAcceptanceCondition}}
   124→<!--
   125→  Format rules (parsers depend on this exact structure):
   126→  - Checkbox line: - [ ] **T01: Title** `est:30m`
   127→  - Description:   indented text on the next line(s)
   128→  - Mark done:     change [ ] to [x]
   129→  - Tasks execute sequentially in order (T01, T02, T03, ...)
   130→  - est: is informational (e.g. 30m, 1h, 2h) and optional
   131→
   132→  Verify field rules:
   133→  - MUST be a mechanically executable command: `npm test`, `grep -q "pattern" file`, `test -f path`
   134→  - MUST NOT use shell pipes, redirects, semicolons, backticks, command substitution, or output trimming
   135→  - MUST NOT use inline `node -e` assertions for verification; put assertions in a real test file and run it with `node --test` or a package test script
   136→  - For content/document tasks: verify file existence, section count, YAML validity, or word count
   137→    NOT exact phrasing, specific formulas, or "zero TBD" aspirational criteria
   138→  - If no command can verify the output, write: "Manual review — file exists and is non-empty"
   139→  - BAD: `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -5`
   140→  - BAD: "Sections 3.1 and 3.2 exist with exact formulas. Zero TBD/TODO."
   141→  - GOOD: `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q --tb=short`
   142→  - GOOD: `grep -c "^## " doc.md` returns >= 4 (4+ sections), `! grep -q "TBD\|TODO" doc.md`
   143→
   144→  Integration closure rule:
   145→  - At least one slice in any multi-boundary milestone should perform real composition/wiring, not just contract hardening
   146→  - For the final assembly slice, verification must exercise the real entrypoint or runtime path
   147→-->
   148→

   152→- `{{filePath}}`

- [MEM071] (pattern) Next.js API routes use App Router: route.ts files export async GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE functions. Collection endpoints at /api/resource/, single resource at /api/resource/[id]/route.ts. Returns NextResponse.json({ data: result }) for success, { error, message } for errors.
- [MEM024] (pattern) Next.js API routes use App Router pattern: route.ts files inside app/api/ directory. Example: app/api/contacts/route.ts for collection endpoints, app/api/contacts/[id]/route.ts for single-resource endpoints. Each route.ts exports async functions: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE. Uses NextRequest/NextResponse types.
- [MEM068] (pattern) ProjectRepository and ProjectApiClient follow DealRepository/DealApiClient pattern exactly. Key differences: Project uses externalNumber (unique string, client-provided) vs Deal.number (auto-generated С-YYYY-NNNNN). Project stages are dynamically created via ProjectStage model, not pipeline-based like Deal.
- [MEM032] (pattern) Standard API routes: GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/resource/[id] for single resources, GET/POST /api/resource for collections. Nested routes for sub-entities like versions/signers.
- [MEM046] (pattern) Prisma include field names use the model field names from schema.prisma (PascalCase like DealStage, Pipeline, Contact, User), not camelCase aliases. API response types (DealData) expect camelCase names (stage, pipeline, contact, manager). Mapping must happen at the API route boundary between Prisma result and JSON response.
- [MEM008] (architecture) Hybrid backend architecture (Next.js API Routes + Python FastAPI) Chose: Next.js API Routes for primary CRUD/auth/UI API; Python FastAPI for background processing (AI, email, Celery tasks). Rationale: Minimizes rewrite (40K+ lines of AI code preserved), provides type-safe end-to-end TypeScript for user-facing operations, leverages Python's superior AI/data ecosystem for intensive processing. Singl….
- [MEM021] (architecture) Milestone M002 slice decomposition strategy Chose: 4 vertical slices: S01 Contact CRUD API (high risk), S02 Contact List UI, S03 Interactions API & UI, S04 Contact Detail & Integration. S01 fully decomposed into 4 tasks (migrations, Prisma wrapper, A…. Rationale: S01 carries highest risk (Prisma migrations + API routes foundation). Proving this works first validates the data layer before UI. S02-S04 are lower risk UI/integration work that depends on working A….
- [MEM055] (gotcha) DealApiClient.url() doesn't serialize pagination params (skip/take); they're declared in DealListParams type but ignored by client — deferred fix
- [MEM030] (architecture) Extract shared API client helpers to dedicated file Chose: Created src/lib/api/shared.ts with ApiClientError, parseApiError, and parseJson; refactored contacts.ts to import from shared instead of duplicating. Rationale: Avoids copy-paste duplication between ContactApiClient and InteractionApiClient. The task allowed either copying or extracting; extracting keeps the codebase DRY and makes it easier to add future API….
- [MEM010] (pattern) Use relative imports for API routes instead of @/* path aliases to avoid tsc type-check issues during development. Next.js runtime handles both, but tsc needs baseUrl configured or explicit relative paths.

1→/**
     2→ * Deals Collection API Endpoint
     3→ *
     4→ * CRUD API for Deal model:
     5→ * - GET: List all deals (excluding soft-deleted) with optional filters
     6→ * - POST: Create a new deal
     7→ *
     8→ * GET /api/deals
     9→ * POST /api/deals
    10→ */
    11→
    12→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
    13→import { deals } from '../../../lib/db/deals'
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * GET /api/deals
    17→ *
    18→ * Returns all active deals (excludes soft-deleted).
    19→ * Supports optional query parameters for filtering.
    20→ */
    21→export async function GET(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    22→  try {
    23→    const searchParams = request.nextUrl.searchParams
    24→    const pipelineId = searchParams.get('pipelineId')
    25→    const stageId = searchParams.get('stageId')
    26→    const managerId = searchParams.get('managerId')
    27→    const contactId = searchParams.get('contactId')
    28→    const status = searchParams.get('status') // For filtering open/closed deals
    29→
    30→    const where: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    31→    if (pipelineId) where.pipelineId = pipelineId
    32→    if (stageId) where.stageId = stageId
    33→    if (managerId) where.managerId = managerId
    34→    if (contactId) where.contactId = contactId
    35→
    36→    // Filter by open/closed status
    37→    if (status === 'open') {
    38→      where.closedAt = null
    39→    } else if (status === 'closed') {
    40→      where.closedAt = { not: null }
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    const allDeals = await deals.findMany({
    44→      where: Object.keys(where).length > 0 ? where : undefined,
    45→      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    46→      include: {
    47→        stage: true,
    48→        pipeline: true,
    49→        contact: true,
    50→        manager: true,
    51→      },
    52→    })
    53→
    54→    return NextResponse.json({ data: allDeals, count: allDeals.length })
    55→  } catch (error) {
    56→    console.error('Failed to fetch deals:', error)
    57→    return NextResponse.json(
    58→      { error: 'Failed to fetch deals', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
    59→      { status: 500 }
    60→    )
    61→  }
    62→}
    63→
    64→/**
    65→ * POST /api/deals
    66→ *
    67→ * Creates a new deal.
    68→ * Validates required fields.
    69→ */
    70→export async function POST(request: NextRequest): Promise<NextResponse> {
    71→  try {
    72→    const body = await request.json()
    73→
    74→    // Validate required fields
    75→    if (!body.title) {
    76→      return NextResponse.json(
    77→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'title is required' },
    78→        { status: 400 }
    79→      )
    80→    }
    81→
    82→    if (!body.pipelineId) {
    83→      return NextResponse.json(
    84→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'pipelineId is required' },
    85→        { status: 400 }
    86→      )
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    if (!body.stageId) {
    90→      return NextResponse.json(
    91→        { error: 'Validation failed', message: 'stageId is required' },
    92→        { status: 400 }
    93→      )
    94→    }
    95→
    96→    // Prepare creation data
    97→    const createData = {
    98→      title: body.title,
    99→      pipelineId: body.pipelineId,
   100→      stageId: body.stageId,
   101→      contactId: body.contactId || null,
   102→      amount: body.amount ?? 0,
   103→      currency: body.currency ?? 'RUB',
   104→      expectedCloseDate: body.expectedCloseDate || null,
   105→      managerId: body.managerId || null,
   106→      description: body.description || null,
   107→      lossReason: body.lossReason || null,
   108→      attributes: body.attributes || null,
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    const newDeal = await deals.create(createData)
   112→
   113→    return NextResponse.json({ data: newDeal }, { status: 201 })
   114→  } catch (error) {
   115→    console.error('Failed to create deal:', error)
   116→    return NextResponse.json(
   117→      { error: 'Failed to create deal', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
   118→      { status: 500 }
   119→    )
   120→  }
   121→}
   122→

686→model Project {
   687→  id              String            @id
   688→  externalNumber  String            @unique
   689→  name            String
   690→  description     String?
   691→  dealId          String?           @unique
   692→  contractId      String?           @unique
   693→  contactId       String?
   694→  status          String            @default("lead")
   695→  contractAmount  Float             @default(0)
   696→  currency        String            @default("RUB")
   697→  startDate       DateTime?
   698→  endDate         DateTime?
   699→  completedAt     DateTime?
   700→  marginTarget    Float             @default(0.25)
   701→  qualityRating   String?
   702→  deadlineStatus  String            @default("on_track")
   703→  managerId       String?
   704→  attributes      Json?
   705→  createdAt       DateTime          @default(now())
   706→  updatedAt       DateTime
   707→  deletedAt       DateTime?
   708→  BOM             BOM?
   709→  Budget          Budget[]
   710→  CashFlowPayment CashFlowPayment[]
   711→  Delivery        Delivery[]
   712→  EmailLog        EmailLog[]
   713→  Event           Event[]
   714→  Invoice         Invoice[]
   715→  User            User?             @relation(fields: [managerId], references: [id])
   716→  Contact         Contact?          @relation(fields: [contactId], references: [id])
   717→  ProjectMember   ProjectMember[]
   718→  ProjectStage    ProjectStage[]
   719→  Production      Production?
   720→  PurchaseRequest PurchaseRequest[]
   721→  Task            Task[]
   722→  Transaction     Transaction[]
   723→
   724→  @@index([contractId])
   725→  @@index([dealId])
   726→  @@index([managerId])
   727→  @@index([status])
   728→}
   729→
   730→model ProjectMember {
   731→  id        String    @id
   732→  projectId String
   733→  userId    String
   734→  role      String
   735→  joinedAt  DateTime  @default(now())
   736→  leftAt    DateTime?
   737→  User      User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
   738→  Project   Project   @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   739→
   740→  @@unique([projectId, userId])
   741→  @@index([userId])
   742→}
   743→
   744→model ProjectStage {
   745→  id          String    @id
   746→  projectId   String
   747→  code        String
   748→  name        String
   749→  order       Int
   750→  status      String    @default("pending")
   751→  startDate   DateTime?
   752→  endDate     DateTime?
   753→  completedAt DateTime?
   754→  assigneeId  String?
   755→  notes       String?
   756→  Project     Project   @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   757→
   758→  @@unique([projectId, code])
   759→  @@index([projectId, order])
   760→}
   761→
   762→model Production {
   763→  id                String            @id
   764→  projectId         String            @unique
   765→  status            String            @default("planning")
   766→  plannedStartDate  DateTime?
   767→  plannedEndDate    DateTime?
   768→  actualStartDate   DateTime?
   769→  actualEndDate     DateTime?
   770→  progress          Float             @default(0)
   771→  notes             String?
   772→  attributes        Json?
   773→  createdAt         DateTime          @default(now())
   774→  updatedAt         DateTime
   775→  deletedAt         DateTime?
   776→  Project           Project          @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
   777→  ProductionStage   ProductionStage[]
   778→
   779→  @@index([status])
   780→  @@index([deletedAt])
   781→  @@index([projectId])
   782→}
   783→
   784→model ProductionStage {
   785→  id              String       @id

1→/**
     2→ * Single Deal API Endpoint
     3→ *
     4→ * CRUD API for a single Deal:
     5→ * - GET: Fetch a deal by ID
     6→ * - PATCH: Update a deal
     7→ * - DELETE: Soft delete a deal
     8→ *
     9→ * GET /api/deals/[id]
    10→ * PATCH /api/deals/[id]
    11→ * DELETE /api/deals/[id]
    12→ */
    13→
    14→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
    15→import { deals } from '@/lib/db/deals'
    16→
    17→interface RouteParams {
    18→  params: Promise<{ id: string }>
    19→}
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * GET /api/deals/[id]
    23→ *
    24→ * Returns a single deal by ID (if not soft-deleted).
    25→ */
    26→export async function GET(
    27→  request: NextRequest,
    28→  { params }: RouteParams
    29→): Promise<NextResponse> {
    30→  try {
    31→    const { id } = await params
    32→
    33→    const deal = await deals.findUnique(id, {
    34→      include: {
    35→        stage: true,
    36→        pipeline: true,
    37→        contact: true,
    38→        manager: true,
    39→        history: {
    40→          orderBy: { changedAt: 'desc' },
    41→          include: {
    42→            fromStage: true,
    43→            toStage: true,
    44→          },
    45→        },
    46→      },
    47→    })
    48→
    49→    if (!deal) {
    50→      return NextResponse.json(
    51→        { error: 'Deal not found', message: `Deal with id ${id} not found` },
    52→        { status: 404 }
    53→      )
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    return NextResponse.json({ data: deal })
    57→  } catch (error) {
    58→    console.error('Failed to fetch deal:', error)
    59→    return NextResponse.json(
    60→      { error: 'Failed to fetch deal', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
    61→      { status: 500 }
    62→    )
    63→  }
    64→}
    65→
    66→/**
    67→ * PATCH /api/deals/[id]
    68→ *
    69→ * Updates an existing deal.
    70→ */
    71→export async function PATCH(
    72→  request: NextRequest,
    73→  { params }: RouteParams
    74→): Promise<NextResponse> {
    75→  try {
    76→    const { id } = await params
    77→    const body = await request.json()
    78→
    79→    // Verify deal exists
    80→    const existing = await deals.findUnique(id)
    81→    if (!existing) {
    82→      return NextResponse.json(
    83→        { error: 'Deal not found', message: `Deal with id ${id} not found` },
    84→        { status: 404 }
    85→      )
    86→    }
    87→
    88→    // Prepare update data (only allow specific fields)
    89→    const updateData: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    90→    if (body.title !== undefined) updateData.title = body.title
    91→    if (body.amount !== undefined) updateData.amount = body.amount
    92→    if (body.currency !== undefined) updateData.currency = body.currency
    93→    if (body.expectedCloseDate !== undefined) updateData.expectedCloseDate = body.expectedCloseDate
    94→    if (body.description !== undefined) updateData.description = body.description
    95→    if (body.lossReason !== undefined) updateData.lossReason = body.lossReason
    96→    if (body.attributes !== undefined) updateData.attributes = body.attributes
    97→    if (body.contactId !== undefined) updateData.contactId = body.contactId
    98→    if (body.managerId !== undefined) updateData.managerId = body.managerId
    99→
   100→    // If stageId is provided, use moveStage instead (to record history)
   101→    if (body.stageId && body.stageId !== existing.stageId) {
   102→      // For stage changes, use the /move endpoint
   103→      return NextResponse.json(
   104→        { error: 'Use /move endpoint', message: 'To change stage, use POST /api/deals/[id]/move' },
   105→        { status: 400 }
   106→      )
   107→    }
   108→
   109→    const updatedDeal = await deals.update(id, updateData)
   110→
   111→    return NextResponse.json({ data: updatedDeal })
   112→  } catch (error) {
   113→    console.error('Failed to update deal:', error)
   114→    return NextResponse.json(
   115→      { error: 'Failed to update deal', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
   116→      { status: 500 }
   117→    )
   118→  }
   119→}
   120→
   121→/**
   122→ * DELETE /api/deals/[id]
   123→ *
   124→ * Soft deletes a deal by setting deletedAt timestamp.
   125→ */
   126→export async function DELETE(
   127→  request: NextRequest,
   128→  { params }: RouteParams
   129→): Promise<NextResponse> {
   130→  try {
   131→    const { id } = await params
   132→
   133→    const deletedDeal = await deals.softDelete(id)
   134→
   135→    return NextResponse.json({ data: deletedDeal })
   136→  } catch (error) {
   137→    console.error('Failed to delete deal:', error)
   138→    return NextResponse.json(
   139→      { error: 'Failed to delete deal', message: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' },
   140→      { status: 500 }
   141→    )
   142→  }
   143→}
   144→

arguments.goal: Implement Project CRUD layer: ProjectRepository, ProjectApiClient, Next.js API routes, and supporting types/tests. This provides the backend foundation for project management before UI development in S03-S04.
arguments.successCriteria:
- ProjectRepository in src/lib/db/projects.ts with CRUD, softDelete, count, and query methods (findByStatus, findByManager, findByContact, findByDeal)
- Repository unit tests (src/lib/db/projects.test.ts) pass: CRUD operations, soft-delete exclusion, count accuracy
- Project API routes at src/app/api/projects/ (GET/POST collection, GET/PATCH/DELETE single resource)
- ProjectApiClient in src/lib/api/projects.ts with getProjects, getProject, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject
- API client tests (src/lib/api/projects.test.ts) pass: fetch serialization, error handling
- Project* types added to src/lib/api/types.ts (ProjectData, ProjectStageData, ProjectMemberData, filters, inputs)
arguments.integrationClosure:
- Upstream: Uses Prisma schema (Project, ProjectStage, ProjectMember models already exist)
- Upstream: Reuses DealRepository/DealApiClient patterns from S01 (deals.ts)
- Upstream: Uses shared utilities (parseApiError, parseJson, ApiClientError from shared.ts)
- New wiring: ProjectRepository connects Prisma to application logic
- New wiring: ProjectApiClient provides typed client for frontend
- New wiring: API routes expose Project CRUD at /api/projects/*
- Before milestone usable: S03 UI needs ProjectApiClient; S04 detail page needs full ProjectData with relations
arguments.observabilityImpact:
Runtime signals: Repository methods log on error (console.error in catch blocks); API routes return structured { error, message } responses; soft-delete preserves records for audit.
Inspection surfaces: GET /api/projects returns all active projects; GET /api/projects/[id] returns single with relations; count() method provides totals.
Failure visibility: 404 for missing projects; 400 for validation failures; 500 for DB errors; soft-delete excludes from queries but preserves data.

tasks.description: Create ProjectRepository class in apps/web/src/lib/db/projects.ts following DealRepository pattern. Includes: findMany (with deletedAt filter), findUnique, findByStatus, findByManager, findByContact, findByDeal, create (with randomUUID for id, manual updatedAt), update (manual updatedAt), softDelete, count, and helper methods for stages (createStage, updateStage) and members (addMember, removeMember, findMembers). Export singleton 'projects' instance.
tasks.title: Create ProjectRepository
tasks.observabilityImpact: Repository logs errors to console; soft-delete preserves records; count method enables totals

tasks.description: Create unit tests for ProjectRepository in apps/web/src/lib/db/projects.test.ts. Test CRUD operations (create, findMany, findUnique, update, softDelete), soft-delete behavior (excluded from queries), count accuracy, and query methods (findByStatus, findByManager, findByContact, findByDeal). Use test fixtures and cleanup between tests. Follow DealRepository.test pattern.
tasks.title: Write ProjectRepository unit tests

tasks.description: Add Project types to apps/web/src/lib/api/types.ts. Add ProjectData (with ProjectStage, ProjectMember, manager, contact, deal, contract relations), ProjectStageData, ProjectMemberData, ProjectFilters, ProjectListParams, ProjectCreateInput, ProjectUpdateInput, ProjectStageCreateInput, ProjectMemberCreateInput. Follow existing DealData, ContactData patterns.
tasks.title: Add Project types to API types

tasks.description: Create Project API routes. Collection endpoint at apps/web/src/app/api/projects/route.ts with GET (list with filters: status, managerId, contactId, dealId) and POST (create with validation: name, externalNumber required). Single resource at apps/web/src/app/api/projects/[id]/route.ts with GET (include stages, members, relations), PATCH (update with existence check), DELETE (soft delete). Follow deals route pattern with NextResponse.json({ data }) success and { error, message } errors.
tasks.title: Create Project API routes

tasks.description: Create ProjectApiClient class in apps/web/src/lib/api/projects.ts following DealApiClient pattern. Include: url() helper, getProjects (with DealListParams-style query), getProject, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject. Use parseApiError, parseJson, ApiClientError from shared.ts. Export singleton 'projectsApi' and convenience exports.
tasks.title: Create ProjectApiClient

tasks.description: Create API client tests in apps/web/src/lib/api/projects.test.ts. Mock fetch for each method (getProjects, getProject, createProject, updateProject, deleteProject). Test successful responses and error handling (404, 400, 500). Follow DealApiClient.test pattern with vi.fn() mocks.
tasks.title: Write ProjectApiClient tests

arguments.choice: externalNumber is client-provided (no auto-generation in API)
arguments.rationale: Project uses externalNumber as a unique string field without @default in schema. Unlike Deal.number (auto-generated С-YYYY-NNNNN), Project numbering may follow external systems (contract numbers, client references). API accepts externalNumber from client; validation enforces uniqueness via @unique constraint. Auto-generation can be added later if needed.

arguments.decision: ProjectStage and ProjectMember API exposure in S02
arguments.choice: ProjectStage/ProjectMember CRUD included in S02 ProjectRepository
arguments.rationale: S02 goal is full Project CRUD layer. ProjectStage and ProjectMember are child entities with onDelete: Cascade to Project. Repository needs methods to manage stages (createStage, updateStage) and members (addMember, removeMember, findMembers) for complete project management. API routes focus on Project root; nested endpoints for stages/members deferred to S04 detail page work.
